criteria.label: State θ̂_MLE = argmax L(θ) and write ℓ(θ) = Σ log P(xᵢ | θ) for i.i.d. data.
criteria.example: For n coin flips: L(p) = p^k(1−p)^(n−k) and ℓ(p) = k log p + (n−k) log(1−p).

criteria.label: Derive a closed-form MLE by setting ∇ℓ(θ) = 0 for a simple model.
Explain why argmax L(θ) equals argmax ℓ(θ) and why practitioners use ℓ.
criteria.example: log is strictly increasing; with 500 data points, ∏ P(xᵢ|θ) underflows but Σ log P(xᵢ|θ) remains finite and differentiable.

criteria.label: Connect a training loss to negative log-likelihood under a named model.
criteria.example: Softmax cross-entropy on true class y is −log P(y | x, θ) under Categorical(softmax(z)).

criteria.label: Implement or interpret numerical MLE via minimizing −ℓ(θ).
criteria.example: Exponential data [1.2, 0.8, 2.1]: scipy minimize of −Σ(log λ − λxᵢ) returns λ̂ ≈ 1/x̄ ≈ 0.87.
